sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.4g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.4g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 3.6g. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be enough nutritional return.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 2.9g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 402% DV, vitamin a at 56% DV, vitamin c at 31% DV stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 402% DV, vitamin a at 56% DV, vitamin c at 31% DV stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 12% DV, magnesium at 19% DV, iron at 15% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 12% DV, magnesium at 19% DV, iron at 15% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. elite micronutrient density because this is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward. very light for the volume because a practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food. easy to use in lots of meals because works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. contains anti-nutrients because oxalates can reduce mineral usefulness in some contexts. volume shrinks a lot when cooked because can make portions feel smaller than expected. not useful as a fuel food because that is fine for vegetables, but it does limit some use cases.

closing.summary: Spinach stands out for elite micronutrient density and very light for the volume. but it also gets held back by contains anti-nutrients and volume shrinks a lot when cooked. Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
